stories.headline: President Christodoulides Announces Developments in Isaac and Solomou Murder Cases
stories.summary: President Nikos Christodoulides stated that there are ongoing developments regarding the 1996 murders of Tasos Isaac and Solomos Solomou, emphasizing the government's commitment to holding those responsible accountable. He made these remarks during a reception for motorcyclists commemorating the 30th anniversary of the tragic events, noting that families would be informed privately.

articles.title: Christodoulides announces ‘developments’ in Isaac, Solomou case

stories.headline: UN Secretary-General Guterres to Visit Cyprus for Peace Talks
stories.summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres is scheduled to visit Cyprus from July 27-29 to meet with Greek Cypriot and Turkish Cypriot leaders, as well as other stakeholders. The visit aims to advance the peace process and support stability on the island, marking Guterres' first official trip to Cyprus as UN chief in 16 years.

stories.headline: Strong Earthquake Strikes Off Mexico, Triggers Tsunami Warning
stories.summary: A powerful magnitude 7.3 earthquake struck off the coast of Mexico's Chiapas state, shaking buildings across Guatemala and El Salvador. While no major damage or casualties were immediately reported, a tsunami warning was issued for coastal areas within 300 km of the epicenter, though it was later lifted.

articles.title: Strong quake off Mexican coast rattles Guatemala and El Salvador, but leaves no damage
Σεισμός στο Μεξικό: Τα κτίρια χορεύουν από τα 7,4 Ρίχτερ και προκαλούν τρόμο – Προειδοποίηση για τσουνάμι – Δείτε βίντεο
Μεξικό: Σεισμός 7,4 βαθμών και προειδοποίηση για τσουνάμι (βίντεο)

stories.headline: Over 100 Homes Destroyed in Norway Wildfire
stories.summary: A large fire near Drammen, Norway, destroyed more than 100 homes as firefighters battled strong winds. Authorities evacuated hundreds of people, and while thick black smoke covered the area, no casualties were reported.

articles.title: More than 100 homes destroyed in Norway fire
Φωτιά σαρώνει οικισμός στη Νορβηγία: Τουλάχιστον 50 σπίτια στις φλόγες – Ακούστηκαν εκρήξεις, εκκενώνεται η περιοχή

stories.headline: Cyprus Awarded 46 Blue Flags for Beaches and Marinas
stories.summary: Cyprus has been awarded 46 Blue Flags for its beaches and two marinas in 2026, recognizing their high environmental quality and sustainable management. Of these, 25 beaches offer facilities for people with disabilities, including eight equipped with the Seatrac system for autonomous sea access.

articles.title: 46 beaches, two marinas currently flying Blue Flag
Γαλάζια Σημαία 2026: 46 κυπριακές παραλίες στη λίστα – Η περιοχή που κυριάρχησε

stories.headline: Supreme Court Allows Challenge to Telecommunications Data Access Order
stories.summary: The Supreme Court has granted permission for an application to challenge the legality of an order allowing access to a suspect's telecommunications data. The court noted a prima facie issue regarding whether the investigated offenses qualify as "serious offenses," a prerequisite for such data access.

articles.title: Supreme Court allows certiorari in illegal data case
Ανώτατο: Άδεια για Certiorari κατά διατάγματος πρόσβασης σε τηλεπικοινωνιακά δεδομένα – Αμφίβολο αν πρόκειται για «σοβαρό αδίκημα»

stories.headline: Appeals Court Upholds 16-Year Sentence for Attempted Murder
stories.summary: The Appeals Court dismissed an appeal against a 16-year prison sentence for a man convicted of two counts of attempted murder in Larnaca. The court affirmed that the initial ruling correctly assessed the seriousness of the offenses, which injured a man and a six-year-old boy, and that the sentences were not excessive.

articles.title: Appeals court upholds attempted murder sentence
«Όχι» από Εφετείο: Στη φυλακή για 16 χρόνια ένοχος για απόπειρα φόνου άντρα και εξάχρονου παιδιού
Court of Appeals upholds 16-year sentences for attempted murder in Larnaca

stories.headline: Police Seize Large Quantities of Cannabis in Cyprus
stories.summary: Cypriot police conducted two separate operations resulting in the seizure of a total of 10.22 kg of cannabis. One operation involved the extradition of a 29-year-old man from Romania in connection with a 2024 case, while the other led to the arrest of a 30-year-old man after 7kg of cannabis arrived by post from abroad.

articles.title: Suspect extradited from Romania over 3kg cannabis case
Police seize 7kg cannabis posted to Cyprus, suspect arrested

stories.headline: EU Commission Highlights Progress and Challenges in Cyprus's Rule of Law
stories.summary: The European Commission's 2026 Rule of Law Report acknowledges Cyprus's progress in judicial and anti-corruption reforms but also points out outstanding issues. Recommendations include completing Legal Service reforms, establishing an Independent Courts Service, and enhancing the operational effectiveness of the Independent Authority against Corruption.

stories.headline: Nicosia Municipality Launches Architectural Competition for Historic Garden Restoration
stories.summary: Nicosia Municipality has initiated an architectural competition for the €4.1 million redevelopment of its historic municipal garden. The project aims to restore and modernize the century-old public space, preserving its architectural identity while enhancing its functionality, accessibility, and environmental friendliness for future generations.

articles.title: Nicosia launches competition to restore historic municipal garden
«Ανάπλαση του Δημοτικού Κήπου Λευκωσίας» - Η Λευκωσία δεν κληρονομεί απλώς την ιστορία της

stories.headline: Cyprus Faces High Fire Risk Amidst Soaring Temperatures
stories.summary: Authorities in Cyprus, including Polis Chrysochous municipality, are urging the public to take extreme caution due to high temperatures increasing the risk of wildfires. A fire in the Kokkinotrimithia buffer zone was brought under control, burning five hectares of vegetation. The Meteorological Department in the TRNC also forecasts temperatures up to 42 degrees Celsius.

articles.title: Polis Chrysochous urges public to help prevent fires
Fire in Kokkinotrimithia buffer zone under control
Hava sıcaklığı 42 dereceye kadar yükselecek

stories.headline: Cyprus Pension Reforms to Bring Higher Incomes by Early 2027
stories.summary: Labour Minister Marinos Mousiouttas announced that pension reforms are on track, with higher payments expected for pensioners starting February 1, 2027. The bill for the first pillar of the pension system will soon be presented to social partners and then submitted to parliament, aiming to ensure the social insurance fund's long-term sustainability and improve pension adequacy.

articles.title: Pensioners to see higher incomes in early 2027
Cyprus Pension Reform Sets Out Increases of 5% to 55%
Landmark pension reform eyes January 2027 rollout with hikes up to 55%
Cyprus targets January 2027 launch for pension reform

stories.headline: Esen Beyköylü Appointed Acting Mayor of Yeniboğaziçi Municipality
stories.summary: Esen Beyköylü has been appointed as the acting mayor of Yeniboğaziçi Municipality following the arrest and imprisonment of the previous mayor, Katip Demir. Beyköylü, a CTP municipal council member, secured the position with 8 votes and will serve until the local elections on December 6.

articles.title: Yeniboğaziçi Belediyesi'nde yeni başkan Esen Beyköylü oldu
Yeniboğaziçi Belediyesi Başkanlığına Esen Beyköylü vekalet edecek
Yeniboğaziçi Belediyesi'nde Yeni Başkan Esen Beyköylü

stories.headline: Father Remains Detained After Death of 3-Year-Old in Paphos Hotel Fall
stories.summary: The father of a three-year-old British boy who died after falling from a hotel window in Paphos remains in custody, despite his lawyer's request for immediate release. The police file has been sent to the Legal Service for instructions, and the father's lawyer has criticized the delay, emphasizing the family's need to inform their five-year-old daughter about her brother's death.

articles.title: Θάνατος τρίχρονου παιδιού: Παραμένει στο κελί ο πατέρας – Έντονος ο δικηγόρος του για τη στάση Αστυνομίας και Νομικής Υπηρεσίας

stories.headline: US Delegation Reviews Cyprus's Progress Towards Visa Waiver Program
stories.summary: A high-level US Department of Homeland Security delegation visited Cyprus to assess the country's advancements in meeting the requirements for the Visa Waiver Program. Discussions with Cypriot officials focused on strengthening border security and combating irregular migration, with Cyprus having completed the technical criteria for inclusion.

articles.title: Πρεσβεία των ΗΠΑ στην Κύπρο: Συζητήθηκε η πρόοδος της Κύπρου για ένταξη στο πρόγραμμα απαλλαγής από Βίζα – Αμερικανοί αξιωματούχοι βρέθηκαν στο νησί

stories.headline: EU Calls on Turkey to Renounce Threats Amidst Strategic Document Release; Turkey Responds
stories.summary: The EU's "Common Understanding" document urges Turkey to commit to good neighborly relations and renounce threats, with the Cyprus problem's resolution being a priority for enhanced EU-Turkey cooperation. Turkey's Foreign Ministry strongly criticized the document, calling its references to Turkey "devoid of a strategic and fair approach" and its claims regarding the Eastern Mediterranean and Cyprus "baseless."

articles.title: Στρατηγικό έγγραφο των «27» της ΕΕ: Καλούν την Τουρκία να αποκηρύξει τις απειλές και να δεσμευθεί για σχέσης καλής γειτονίας

stories.headline: Ukrainian-Cypriot Oligarch Accuses Ukrainian Intelligence of Monaco Bomb Attack
stories.summary: Vadym Iermolaiev, a Ukrainian-born businessman with Cypriot citizenship, has publicly accused Ukraine's military intelligence service (GUR) of being behind a bomb attack in Monaco that seriously injured him, his partner, and their son. The alleged bomber was later found dead in Ukraine, and two suspects with ties to Ukrainian intelligence have been arrested in connection with her death.

stories.headline: Turkish Cypriot Government Faces Scrutiny Over Citizenship Grants
stories.summary: The Turkish Cypriot Council of Ministers has granted citizenship to 131 individuals, while reports suggest the government is preparing to grant exceptional citizenship to another 260 people before local elections. This move has drawn criticism from unions regarding the timing and potential political motivations.

articles.title: Bakanlar Kurulu'ndan 131 kişiye vatandaşlık
"Hükümet, 260 kişiye istisnai yurttaşlık vermeye hazırlanıyor"

stories.headline: Debates on Patient Rights and Health System in Northern Cyprus
stories.summary: Several opinion pieces from Yeni Duzen discuss the state of patient rights and the health system in Northern Cyprus. Authors emphasize that health is a fundamental human right, not a privilege, and call for stronger institutions, scientific planning, and effective oversight to ensure quality and accessible healthcare.

articles.title: “Ulusal tabip birliklerine her zamankinden daha çok ihtiyaç duyuluyor”

stories.headline: "Billboard Crisis" Exposes Cracks in Turkish Cypriot Coalition Government
stories.summary: A "billboard crisis" has reportedly widened a rift within the Turkish Cypriot coalition government. Transport Minister Erhan Arıklı deflected questions about the Antik Liman pier to the UBP, stating that the UBP's billboards claimed sole credit for its renovation, implying a dispute over political ownership and responsibility.

articles.title: “O zaman o makamda niye oturuyorsun?”
Billboard krizi koalisyonda çatlağı büyüttü!

stories.headline: Cypriot Football Clubs Announce New Player Signings
stories.summary: Cypriot football clubs Omonia and Apollon Limassol have been active in the transfer market. Omonia is set to officially announce the signing of 33-year-old French forward Lois Diony, a personal choice of coach Henning Berg, while Apollon Limassol has secured the transfer of central defender Emmanuel Agyemang from Austrian Sturm Graz.

articles.title: Έσκασαν ανακοίνωση για Ντιονί / Έφερε αυτόν που ήθελε ο Μπεργκ

stories.headline: International News Roundup
stories.summary: A collection of international news stories includes Marvel Comics relocating to California, Israeli strikes in Gaza, SpaceX's Starship launch attempt, Netflix missing earnings targets, and a parasite found in fast food lettuce causing an intestinal epidemic.

stories.headline: Diverse Cultural and Community Events Across Cyprus
stories.summary: Cyprus is hosting a variety of local events, including "Cinema by the Sea" screenings at beaches in Limassol, a successful theatrical performance in Drouseia, and the conclusion of Agia Marina celebrations in Agia Marina Chrysochous. In the Turkish Cypriot community, the Lefkoşa Turkish Municipality organized a Children's Festival in the Walled City.

articles.title: Cinema by the sea arrives at Cyprus beaches

stories.headline: Technical Issues Delay Konia Roadworks Completion
stories.summary: The completion of roadworks in Konia, Paphos, has been delayed by a few days due to technical problems encountered by the contractor during asphalt laying. Paphos acting mayor Angelos Onisiforou assured that the municipality is closely monitoring the works to ensure a high-quality and safe project.

articles.title: Konia road to open to traffic on Tuesday

stories.headline: Güzelyurt Municipality Begins Construction of "Life Center" for Seniors
stories.summary: Güzelyurt Municipality has commenced construction on its "Life Center" project, designed to support the social lives and promote active and healthy aging for citizens aged 60 and over. The initiative aims to provide a dedicated space for seniors in the community.

articles.title: Güzelyurt'taki "Yaşam Merkezi" projesi inşaat aşamasına geçti

stories.headline: Traffic Closures in Lefke for Upcoming Triathlon Race
stories.summary: Temporary traffic closures will be implemented at intersections along the triathlon route in Lefke on Sunday due to an upcoming race. Motorists are advised to be aware of the disruptions.

articles.title: Lefke’de, triatlon yarışı sırasında güzergahtaki kavşaklar trafiğe kapatılacak

stories.headline: Kıb-Tek Continues Construction of New Service Buildings
stories.summary: Construction work on Kıb-Tek's new service building projects is proceeding without interruption. These new facilities are part of ongoing efforts to enhance the infrastructure of the Turkish Cypriot Electricity Authority.

articles.title: Kıb-Tek’in yeni hizmet binası yapımları devam ediyor

stories.headline: Ecologists Movement Denounces Animal Cruelty in Lakatamia
stories.summary: The Ecologists Movement has raised serious allegations of animal abuse in Lakatamia, citing multiple incidents of cats and dogs being shot on rooftops since 2024. The movement calls for immediate action from authorities to investigate these cases and prevent further cruelty.

articles.title: Δύο χρόνια καταγγελίες χωρίς αποτέλεσμα – Πυροβολούν γατιά και σκυλιά σε ταράτσα

stories.headline: Online Shopping from Temu and Shein Remains Popular Despite New Fees
stories.summary: Despite the imposition of a €3 fee per product category, online shopping from platforms like Temu and Shein continues to be popular among Cypriot consumers. Data from the Customs Department indicates that state revenues from these purchases have exceeded one million euros in the initial period.

articles.title: Τα €3 δεν έβαλαν «φρένο» στις παραγγελίες – Τα στοιχεία που προκαλούν εντύπωση – «Βροχή» παραγγελιών από Temu και Shein

stories.headline: Miscellaneous Local News from Cyprus
stories.summary: This category includes a range of local news items such as a masterplan for Pegeia's traditional core, President Christodoulides' vision for Cyprus to host the World Cup, DIKO's commitment to a viable Cyprus solution, and the Palestinian Ambassador to Cyprus discussing a sensational plan for Israeli prisons.

articles.title: Εκπονούν masteplan για την ολική ανάπλαση του παραδοσιακού πυρήνα της Πέγειας
Δεν κάνει πίσω ο Πρόεδρος για το Μουντιάλ: «Ναι, μπορούμε να το διεκδικήσουμε»
Παλαιστίνιος Πρέσβης στην Κύπρο: «Σχέδιο-σοκ για τάφρους με κροκοδείλους γύρω από ισραηλινές φυλακές»
Παπαδόπουλος: Το ΔΗΚΟ προσηλωμένο σε βιώσιμη λύση

stories.headline: Turkish Cypriot Political Parties Discuss Elections and Coalition Issues
stories.summary: The UBP's Central Executive Board has proposed holding local and early general elections concurrently, a move that has reportedly widened cracks in the coalition government. Transport Minister Erhan Arıklı also criticized the UBP over a "billboard crisis" regarding credit for the Antik Liman renovation.

articles.title: UBP MYK'dan "iki seçim birlikte" önerisi
“O zaman o makamda niye oturuyorsun?”
Billboard krizi koalisyonda çatlağı büyüttü!

stories.headline: Diverse Perspectives from Turkish Cypriot Columnists
stories.summary: This collection of opinion pieces from Yeni Duzen covers a range of topics including reflections on historical events, the impact of technology, and personal essays.

articles.title: Hakkı Yücel: O Gece… Mum Işığında…!
Ali Furkan Çetiner: Kiralık İnternet – Çocukluğumdan Bugüne
Akile Ruh: Kangren sevdam
Eğri otur doğru konuş; Türkiye neden çözüm istesin?

stories.headline: Two Deaths Reported in Turkish Cypriot Community
stories.summary: Two separate incidents resulted in deaths in the Turkish Cypriot community. A 36-year-old man, Gökhan Yücel, passed away after falling ill in a hotel room in Famagusta, and an 84-year-old man, Osman Polatcan, died from injuries sustained in a work accident involving a hand hoe machine in Güzelyurt.

articles.title: Otel odasında rahatsızlanan 36 yaşındaki Gökhan Yücel yaşamını yitirdi
El çapası makinesine kapılan 84 yaşındaki Osman Polatcan yaşamını yitirdi
